title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers cloudfront cache keys, origin requests, ttls, and invalidation into a new cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 1385 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Cache Key Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Cache Key Identity, keep Cache Key Identity connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compression Vary, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, custody, integrity, range, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 3718 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compression Vary, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 5337 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

stimulus.title: Compression Vary failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Compression Vary condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Compression Vary happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend the transferred model · Lab Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Compression Vary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 1908 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.
